channel.title: Maine Secretary of State — Elections Division News
channel.description: Elections hub page is static; election-specific announcements (recounts, RCV tabulations) are posted to the sitewide SOS news feed.

item.title: Secretary of State issues decision in challenge to Kevin Nichols nomination petitions for Androscoggin County Sheriff
item.description:
Secretary of State issues decision in challenge to Kevin Nichols nomination petitions for Androscoggin County Sheriff jana.spaulding… Wed, 06/10/2026 - 20:40

6/10/2026

AUGUSTA, Maine — Secretary of State Shenna Bellows issued a decision regarding a challenge to the nomination petitions filed by Kevin Nichols as a non-party candidate for Androscoggin County Sheriff.

The Secretary of State’s Office received the written challenge from Eric G. Samson alleging that Mr. Nichols has no recorded history of holding a supervisory position as required by Maine statute 30-A M.R.S. §371-B (3)(E). A hearing was held on Wednesday, June 3 with Deputy Secretary Julie Flynn of the Bureau of Corporations, Elections and Commissions serving as the Presiding Officer at the Hearing, and Assistant Attorney General Jon Bolton assisting as legal counsel.

The Secretary, upon review of the proceedings and evidence, determined that Samson met his burden to establish that Nichols lacks the required supervisory employment experience.

“Based on the proceedings and the evidence, I agree with the conclusion and recommendation submitted by the hearings officer that Mr. Nichols lacks the supervisory experience required by law to hold this position.”

item.title: Maine Secretary of State’s Office announces additional races for Ranked Choice Voting tabulations, recounts
item.description:
Maine Secretary of State’s Office announces additional races for Ranked Choice Voting tabulations, recounts jana.spaulding… Tue, 06/16/2026 - 08:59

6/16/2026

AUGUSTA, MAINE – The Department of the Secretary of State announced today two additional races will proceed to ranked choice voting tabulation and three to recounts:

Ranked choice tabulation:

  • House District 58 – Republican (Belgrade, Fayette, Rome, Vienna, Mount Vernon, New Sharon)
  • Senate District 4 – Republican (Bradford; Charleston, Corinna, Corinth, Dexter, Exeter, Garland, Glenburn, Hudson, Kenduskeag, Levant)
  • Governor – Democrat
  • Governor – Republican 
  • U.S. Congress CD2 – Democrat 

Recounts:

  • Senate District 30 – Democrat (Gorham, Scarborough (part))
  • House District 49 – Republican (Arrowsic, Georgetown, Phippsburg, West Bath, Woolwich)
  • House District 135 – Democrat  (Kennebunk (part))

The ranked choice tabulation process began on Friday, June 12 at 1 PM at the Maine Department of Public Safety Headquarters building located at 45 Commerce Drive in Augusta and is expected to conclude this week. The ranked choice process is open to the public and media and is being livestreamed on the Secretary of State’s YouTube page. Recounts will begin next week with dates to be announced later this week.

How Ranked Choice Voting works in Maine: In Ranked Choice Voting races, a candidate must receive more than 50% of the first-choice votes as counted on Election Day to win the race outright. If no candidate appears to have achieved this based on initial unofficial results reports from

item.title: Maine Secretary of State’s Office announces recount schedule
item.description:
Maine Secretary of State’s Office announces recount schedule jana.spaulding… Tue, 06/16/2026 - 12:13

6/16/2026

AUGUSTA, MAINE – The Department of the Secretary of State announced the schedule for recounts requested by candidates in three races:

Recounts:

  • Monday, June 22 at 9 a.m.: Senate District 30 – Democrat 
  • Thursday, June 25 at 9 a.m.: House District 49 – Republican
  • Thursday, June 25 at 1 p.m.: House District 135 – Democrat  

The recounts will take place in the Florian Room located at the Maine Public Safety Building at 45 Commerce Ave., Augusta. Recounts are open to the public and media and will be livestreamed on the Secretary of State’s YouTube page.

Additional recounts could be requested following the conclusion of the ranked choice voting tabulations.

Recount process: Maine statute Title 21-A, §737-A lays out a specific plan, process and any related costs for votes to be recounted. In a recount, representatives of each candidate and staff members from the Department of the Secretary of State manually review each paper ballot to determine the official vote tally:

  • In Ranked Choice Voting races: only a candidate who received one of the top three rankings at the end of the penultimate round of ranked‑choice counting may request a recount, and they must file with the Secretary of State a written request within five business days after results are announced.  
  • In non-Ranked Choice races: any candidate

item.title: Maine Secretary of State issues decision in challenge to Albert Joy nomination petitions for Knox County Sheriff
item.description:
Maine Secretary of State issues decision in challenge to Albert Joy nomination petitions for Knox County Sheriff jana.spaulding… Thu, 06/18/2026 - 17:02

6/18/2026

AUGUSTA, Maine — Secretary of State Shenna Bellows issued a decision regarding a challenge to the nomination petitions filed by Albert Joy as a non-party candidate for Knox County Sheriff. Secretary Bellows has rejected the challenge, finding Mr. Joy eligible for the November ballot.

The Secretary of State’s Office received the written challenge from Daniel Landers alleging that Mr. Joy did not meet the statutory qualifications for the office of sheriff pursuant to Maine law. A hearing was held on Thursday, June 11 with Chief Deputy Secretary Katherine McBrien serving as the Presiding Officer at the hearing, and Assistant Attorney General Jon Bolton assisting as legal counsel. A recording on the hearing can be viewed on the Secretary of State’s YouTube page: https://www.youtube.com/@mesecofstate. 

The Secretary, upon review of the proceedings and evidence, the recommendation of the he

item.title: Maine Secretary of State’s Office announces ranked choice tabulations
item.description:
Maine Secretary of State’s Office announces ranked choice tabulations jana.spaulding… Fri, 06/19/2026 - 02:10

6/19/2026

AUGUSTA, MAINE – The Elections division today completed ranked choice tabulations on five June 9, 2026 Primary Election races. Based on ranked choice tabulations, final results for each race are as follows (Note: click the links for round-by-round details):

Governor – Republican

  • Winner: Bobby Charles
  • Eliminated by round: 1-Libby, 2-Wessels, 3-Jones, 4-McCarthy, 5-Mason, 6-Bush 
  • Runner-up Midgley

Governor – Democrat 

  • Winner: Hannah Pingree
  • Eliminated by round: 1-King, 2-Bellows, 3-Jackson 
  • Runner-Up: Shah

U.S. Congress CD2 – Democrat 

  • Winner: Matthew Dunlap
  • Eliminated by round: 1-Loud, 2-Wood
  • Runner-Up: Baldacci

Senate District 4 – Republican (Bradford; Charleston, Corinna, Corinth, Dexter, Exeter, Garland, Glenburn, Hudson, Kenduskeag, Levant)

  • Winner: Chad Perkins
  • Eliminated by round: 1-Pierce, 2-Wood
  • Runner-Up: Guerin

House District 58 – Republican (Belgrade

item.title: Maine Secretary of State’s Office announces result of Senate District 30 recount
item.description:
Maine Secretary of State’s Office announces result of Senate District 30 recount jana.spaulding… Mon, 06/22/2026 - 16:48

6/22/2026

AUGUSTA, MAINE – The Department of the Secretary of State announced the results for a recount for Maine Senate District 30 Democrat (Gorham and part of Scarborough):

  • Warren, Sophia Bailey – 3,838 
  • Sato, Eleanor Y. – 3,807 

Representative Ellie Sato of Gorham requested a recount after Representative Sophia Warren of Scarborough was declared the winner with a 29-vote margin. District 30 is currently represented by Senator Stacy Brenner, who did not seek reelection.

Two additional recounts, for House District 49 Republican and House District 135 Democrat, are scheduled for this Thursday. Recounts are open to the public and media and are livestreamed on the Secretary of State’s YouTube page.

Recount process: Maine statute Title 21-A, §737-A lays out a specific plan, process and any related costs for votes to be recounted. In a recount, representatives of each candidate and staff members from the Department of the Secretary of State manually review each paper ballot to determine the official vote tally: 

  • In non-Ranked Choice races: any candidate other than the apparent winner who desires a recount must file with the Secretary of State a written request for a recount within five business days after the election. 
  • In Ranked Choice Voting races: only a candidate who r

item.title: Secretary of State Shenna Bellows statement on federal court striking down provisions of anti-voting Executive Order
item.description:
Secretary of State Shenna Bellows statement on federal court striking down provisions of anti-voting Executive Order jana.spaulding… Wed, 06/24/2026 - 16:28

6/24/2026

AUGUSTA, MAINE – Secretary of State Shenna Bellows issued the following statement in response to Chief U.S. District Judge Denise Casper permanently enjoining provisions of a 2025 anti-voting Executive Order by President Trump. 

“Donald Trump continues to stack up loss after loss in court, and as we are now just days away from the 250th anniversary of the birth of our nation, states continue to show that when we stand united, we can successfully defend the rule of law and our rights to administer elections. 

The judge could not have stated it more plainly – the U.S. Constitution assigns control over elections to the States and does not grant the president any powers over elections. We will continue to fight Donald Trump and the DOJ’s every attempt to interfere with free and fair elections.”

The ruling comes in response to a 19-state litigation challenging the order and stated, “there is no evidence in this record of widespread “illegal voting, discrimination, fraud, and other forms of malfeasance and error” within American elections, which the Executive Order purports to safeguard against.”

### 

Attachment: Memorandum and Order

item.title: Maine Secretary of State’s Office announces results of House District 49 recount
item.description:
Maine Secretary of State’s Office announces results of House District 49 recount jana.spaulding… Thu, 06/25/2026 - 11:35

6/25/2026

AUGUSTA, MAINE – The Department of the Secretary of State announced the results for a recount for House District 49 – Republican (Arrowsic, Georgetown, Phippsburg, West Bath, Woolwich):

  • √  Suzanne Andresen – 608 
  •  Nicolas Hamlin – 602 

Nicolas Hamlin of West Bath requested a recount after Suzanne Andresen of West Bath was declared the winner with an 8-vote (609-601) margin. District 49 is currently represented by Representative Allison Hepler (D-Woolwich).  

House District 135 Democrat is also scheduled for a recount today. Recounts are open to the public and media and are livestreamed on the Secretary of State’s YouTube page.

Recount process: Maine statute Title 21-A, §737-A lays out a specific plan, process and any related costs for votes to be recounted. In a recount, representatives of each candidate and staff members from the Department of the Secretary of State manually review each paper ballot to determine the official vote tally:

  • In non-Ranked Choice races: any candidate other than the apparent winner who desires a recount must file with the Secretary of State a written request for a recount within five business days after the election. 
  • In Ranked Choice Voting races: only a candidate who received one of the top three rankings at the end of the penultimate round of

item.title: Secretary of State Shenna Bellows statement on Postmaster General’s declaration USPS will refuse to deliver election mail
item.description:
Secretary of State Shenna Bellows statement on Postmaster General’s declaration USPS will refuse to deliver election mail  jana.spaulding… Thu, 06/25/2026 - 13:37

6/25/2026

AUGUSTA, MAINE –Secretary of State Shenna Bellows issued the following statement in response to Postmaster General David Steiner declaration to lawmakers Wednesday that the U.S. Postal Service will decline to deliver mail-in ballots in states that refuse to provide sensitive voter data to the federal government, despite numerous court decisions ruling in the states’ favor. Today, U.S. District Judge Indira Talwani ruled that the federal government cannot decide who can vote by mail.

“Never in the history of the United States has the U.S. Postal Service declined to mail ballots for political reasons. In Maine, many voters including seniors, people with disabilities and Mainers working multiple jobs rely on the mail to get and return absentee ballots,” said Secretary Bellows. “The idea that the U.S. Postal Service would refuse to deliver any ballots in a state where we have properly refused the Trump Administration’s demands for voter data is an illegal and unconstitutional attempt to keep Americans from voting. We’ve won every court case on this issue, and we’re not going to back down now in defending Mainers’ voting rights.” 

According to federal law: “The Postal Service shall have as its basic function the obligation to provide postal services to bind the Nation together through the personal, educational, literary, and business correspondence of the peo

item.title: Maine Secretary of State’s Office announces results of House District 135 recount
item.description:
Maine Secretary of State’s Office announces results of House District 135 recount jana.spaulding… Thu, 06/25/2026 - 16:05

6/25/2026

AUGUSTA, MAINE – The Department of the Secretary of State announced the results for a recount for House District 135 – Democrat (part of Kennebunk):

  • √  Lisa Pratt – 1,151
  • Rachel Phipps – 1,133

Rachel Phipps of Kennebunk requested the recount after Lisa Pratt, also of Kennebunk, was declared the winner by a 19-vote margin following the June 9 Primary Election. District 135 is currently represented by Daniel Sayre (D-Kennebunk) who did not seek reelection. 

Recounts are open to the public and media and are livestreamed on the Secretary of State’s YouTube page.

Recount process: Maine statute Title 21-A, §737-A lays out a specific plan, process and any related costs for votes to be recounted. In a recount, representatives of each candidate and staff members from the Department of the Secretary of State manually review each paper ballot to determine the official vote tally:

  • In non-Ranked Choice races: any candidate other than the apparent winner who desires a recount must file with the Secretary of State a written request for a recount within five business days after the election. 
  • In Ranked Choice Voting races: only a candidate who received one of the top three rankings at the end of the penultimate round of ranked‑choice counting may request a recount, and they must file wit
